data.title: RED: Add test asserting eval guard absent behavior in record-test-status.sh
data.description:
## Testing Mode
UPDATE

## TDD Requirement
This task WRITES the RED test ONLY. Do NOT modify record-test-status.sh or delete any files.

Write a new test case in tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part4.sh that:
1. Sets up a temp git repo with a staged incomplete promptfooconfig.yaml (missing llm-rubric assertion)
2. Runs record-test-status.sh with this staged file
3. Asserts exit code 0 — the eval guard should NOT block the commit

Currently record-test-status.sh exits 1 for this scenario (the guard fires at lines 401-473), so the test will FAIL (RED). After T2 removes the guard, the test will PASS (GREEN).

## Implementation Notes
- Add the new test case to the existing test file: tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part4.sh
- Use the same test framework pattern as existing tests in that file (sourcing test helpers, temp repo setup)
- Test function name suggestion: test_record_status_eval_guard_absent or similar
- The incomplete promptfooconfig.yaml should contain a valid tests: list but omit the type: llm-rubric assertion

## File Impact
| Source file | Action | Associated tests | Test classification |
|-------------|--------|-----------------|---------------------|
| tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part4.sh | modify | N/A (this IS the test) | N/A |

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0)
  Verify: bash tests/run-all.sh
- [ ] ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] ruff format --check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py passes (exit 0)
  Verify: ruff format --check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py
- [ ] test-record-test-status-part4.sh contains new test case asserting eval guard absence
  Verify: grep -q "eval.*guard\|guard.*absent\|incomplete.*promptfoo" tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part4.sh
- [ ] The new test case fails (RED) against current code — verified manually by running the test file and observing the new test fails while others pass
  Verify: (manual) bash tests/hooks/test-record-test-status-part4.sh 2>&1 | grep "FAIL"
